name: vskernels

metadata.name: vskernels
metadata.summary: Kernel objects for scaling and format conversion within VapourSynth

documentation:
# vs-kernels

Kernels are a collection of wrappers pertaining to (de)scaling, format conversion, and other related operations, all while providing a consistent and clean interface. This allows for easy expansion and ease of use for any other maintainers who wishes to use them in their own functions. You can create presets for common scaling algorithms or settings, while ensuring the interface will always remain the same, even across different plugins with their own settings and expected behavior. For support you can check out the [JET Discord server](https://discord.gg/XTpc6Fa9eB).

## How to install Install `vskernels` with the following command: ```sh pip install vskernels --no-cache-dir -U ``` Or if you want the latest git version, install it with this command: ```sh pip install git+https://github.com/Jaded-Encoding-Thaumaturgy/vs-kernels.git --no-cache-dir -U ```

classes.name: vskernels.kernels.bicubic.Bicubic
documentation:
Built-in bicubic resizer.

Default: b=0, c=0.5

```
param b

B-param for bicubic kernel

param c

C-param for bicubic kernel
```

documentation: Bicubic b=0, c=0.6; FFmpeg's swscale default

classes.name: vskernels.kernels.bicubic.AdobeBicubicSmoother
documentation: Bicubic b=0, c=0.625, blur=1.15; Adobe's "Bicubic Smoother" interpolation preset.

Kernel that follows the rule of:
b + 2c = target

classes.name: vskernels.util.NoShift

methods.name: vskernels.util.NoShift.from_kernel
documentation:
Function or decorator for making a kernel not shift.

As example, in vsaa:
```
doubled_no_shift = Znedi3(..., shifter=NoShift.from_kernel('lanczos')).scale(...)

# which in *this case* can also be written as this
doubled_no_shift = Znedi3(..., shifter=NoShift, scaler=Lanczos).scale(...)
```

Or for some other code:
```
@NoShift.from_kernel
class CustomCatromWithoutShift(Catrom):
    # some cool code
    ...
```

Class util used to always pass shift=(0, 0)

By default it inherits from {py:class}`vskernels.Bicubic`,
this behaviour can be changed with {py:attr}`Noshift.from_kernel`

Use case, for example vsaa's ZNedi3:
`` `
test = ...  # some clip, 480x480
doubled_no_shift = Znedi3(field=0, nsize=4, nns=3, shifter=NoShift).scale(test, 960, 960)
down = Point.scale(double, 480, 480)
` ``

classes.name: vskernels.kernels.docs.Example

methods.name: vskernels.kernels.docs.Example.scale
documentation:
Perform a regular scaling operation.

```
param clip

Input clip

param width

Output width

param height

Output height

param shift

Shift clip during the operation.
Expects a tuple of (src_top, src_left).

rtype

VideoNode
```

methods.name: vskernels.kernels.docs.Example.descale
documentation:
Perform a regular descaling operation.

```
param clip

Input clip

param width

Output width

param height

Output height

param shift

Shift clip during the operation.
Expects a tuple of (src_top, src_left).

rtype

VideoNode
```

methods.name: vskernels.kernels.docs.Example.resample
documentation:
Perform a regular resampling operation.

```
param clip

Input clip

param format

Output format

param matrix

Output matrix. If , will take the matrix from the input clip's frameprops.

param matrix_in

Input matrix. If , will take the matrix from the input clip's frameprops.

rtype

VideoNode
```

methods.name: vskernels.kernels.docs.Example.shift
documentation:
Perform a regular shifting operation.

```
param clip

Input clip

param shift

Shift clip during the operation.

Expects a tuple of (src_top, src_left)

or two top, left arrays for shifting planes individually.

rtype

VideoNode
```
Example Kernel class for documentation purposes.
